prompt_rules: cohesive 4x4 terrain transition tileset for grass-to-path or grass-to-stone-road boundaries
include center road variants, four edge tiles, four outer corners, inner corners, chipped edges, grass-overlap, and small decoration variants
avoid straight rectangular bands; make edges broken, organic, and SFC-style while remaining tilemap-compatible
do not draw grid lines, labels, or tile numbers

prompt:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette: DEEP rich saturated greens (early-summer rice-field banks), clear blue-green water, vermilion and white accents, soft warm sunlight. NOT dry, NOT yellow-brown, NOT a western prairie or wasteland. Top-down transition TILESET: a packed light-earth footpath through deep green satoyama grass - moist brown soil with faint stepping-stone and pebble hints, soft organic grass-edge transitions (no straight hard lines). STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT: row1 = four full CENTER variations, row2 = edge tiles with the neighbour terrain on the North / South / West / East side, row3 = outer corner tiles NW / NE / SW / SE, row4 = four accent center variations. Each cell completely filled edge to edge, tiles seamlessly tileable, no borders or gaps between cells, no text.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
